description: Vol 2 Second Reconstruction Period Started In Southern States By U S Judiciary Legion Told Nation Facing Its Most Deadly Peril Talmadge Scores Brownells All inclusive Civil Rights Bill Status Of Forces Treaty Should Be Repudiated Georgias Senator Declares Herman Talmadge told the American Legion convention in Augusta that the nation today faces its most deadly peril That threat is the sustained and vic ious attacks now being waged on two broad fronts the objective of which is the utter annihilation of our system of representative constitutional government in this country says Talmadge One of these perils is aimed at destroy ing constitutional processes and the other is aimed at bankrupting the nation Would Be Final Should either succeed Talmadge says It will succeed with finality The main portion of his speech follows On no occasion has the American Leg ion ever failed to stand up and speak out vigorously in defense of our institu tions and ideals and for maintenance of our guarantees of liberty and freedom It has done so courageously sometimes in face of severe hostility and at actual peril to the organization itself The undeviating record of the Legion therefore has been and ever will be
